Japan 1Q Data May Show GDP Shrunk for First Time in a Year — Market Talk
data.description: The Japanese economy may have shrunk for the first time in a year during the first three months of 2025, government data due Friday is likely to show. Japan's real GDP is expected to have contracted 0.1% in the January-March period from the previous quarter, versus the 0.6% growth recorded in the October-December period, according to a poll of economists by data provider Quick. External demand likely weighed on the economy as imports increased more than exports, economists say. They expect capital expenditure to have risen in the first quarter but companies may be growing more cautious about spending due to fears about the impact of higher U.S. tariffs. (megumi.fujikawa@wsj.com)

data.title: Global Forex and Fixed Income Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering FX and Fixed Income. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

The Japanese economy may have shrunk for the first time in a year during the first three months of 2025, government data due Friday is likely to show. Japan's real GDP is expected to have contracted 0.1% in the January-March period from the previous quarter, versus the 0.6% growth recorded in the October-December period, according to a poll of economists by data provider Quick. External demand likely weighed on the economy as imports increased more than exports, economists say. They expect capital expenditure to have risen in the first quarter but companies may be growing more cautious about spending due to fears about the impact of higher U.S. tariffs. (megumi.fujikawa@wsj.com)

data.title: Litchfield Minerals Says Assay Results Delayed Due to Logistics and Laboratory Issues
data.description:
Litchfield Minerals said assay results were delayed due to "unforeseen logistical and laboratory issues," according to a Wednesday Australian bourse filing.

The samples were collected around one week after drilling concluded, however, logistical issues on the part of the transport company resulted in a two-week delay in delivering the samples to a South Australian laboratory.

The laboratory said that its analytical equipment experienced operational issues, and the samples were redirected to a facility in Western Australia, where they are expected to arrive on Friday.

The Western Australia lab indicated that final assay results will be available around two weeks after delivery.

data.title: KBC Group Posts Higher Q1 Result; Total Income Up
data.description:
KBC Group on Thursday posted a higher result and total income for the first quarter.

Result attributable to equity holders of the parent for the three months ended March 31 was 546 million euros, compared with 506 million euros earlier. EPS moved to 1.32 euros from 1.18 euros.

The insurer's total income was 2.92 billion euros, compared with 2.71 billion euros a year ago.

For 2025, KBC expects total income to rise at least 5.5% year over year.

data.title: Indian army operation on Myanmar border kills 10 insurgents
data.description:
The Indian Army said at least 10 militants were killed in an operation still underway in the troubled northeastern state of Manipur, bordering Myanmar.

The deaths came months after Reuters reported in November that Indian militant groups that sought refuge in Myanmar and fought in its civil war had begun streaming back across the border to Manipur.

"Ten cadres were neutralised and a sizeable quantity of arms and ammunition have been recovered," the army said in a post on X on Wednesday, referring to a firefight in the region.

Stability has been at risk along the shared 1,650-km (1,025-mile) border from the civil war in Myanmar after a military coup ousted an elected civilian government in February 2021.

On the Indian side, ethnic strife in Manipur since May 2023 has led to the deaths of nearly 260, with more than 60,000 displaced.

The state of 3.2 million has been divided into two ethnic enclaves, a valley controlled by the Meiteis and the Kuki-dominated hills.

Slovenia books 446 mln euro c/a surplus in Q1
data.description:
LJUBLJANA (Slovenia), May 15 (SeeNews) - Slovenia recorded a current account surplus of 446 million euro ($500.6 million) in the first quarter of 2025, down from a surplus of 677.5 million euro in the like period of last year, the central bank said.

Trade in goods was in surplus of 18.8 million euro in the first quarter of 2025, while the surplus in trade in services was 708 million euro, the central bank said on Wednesday, citing preliminary figures.

Primary income deficit amounted to 52.7 million euro in January-March 2025, whereas the deficit in secondary income came in at 228 million euro.

In 2024, the current account surplus amounted to 3 billion euro, higher than 2.9 billion euro in 2023.

($ = 0.891 euro)

data.title: North Macedonia's pension funds assets edge down in Apr
data.description:
SKOPJE (N. Macedonia), May 15 (SeeNews) - The net assets managed by North Macedonia's three mandatory pension funds came in at 165 billion denars ($3 billion/2.7 billion euro) in April, down 0.3% compared with the end of the previous month, official data showed.

KB Prv Otvoren Zadolzitelen Penziski Fond (KBPz) ranked first with 80.3 billion denars in net assets at the end of April, followed by Sava Penziski Fond (SAVAz) with 71.4 billion denars and Triglav Otvoren Zadolzitelen Penziski Fond (TRIGLAVz) with 13.4 billion denars, according to figures released by North Macedonia's pension insurance supervision agency, MAPAS, earlier this week.

KBPz had 282,795 clients at the end of April, SAVAz account holders numbered 263,054 and TRIGLAVz clients totalled 70,463.

KBPz is managed by KB Prvo Penzisko Drustvo, co-owned by Slovenia's Skupina Prva and North Macedonia's Komercijalna Banka, according to MAPAS data.

SAVAz, managed by Sava Penzisko Drustvo, is owned by Slovenian reinsurer Sava Re, whereas TRIGLAVz is managed by Triglav Penzisko Drustvo and owned by Slovenian insurer Triglav.

North Macedonia has a population of 1.8 million.

(1 euro = 61.52 denars)

data.title: Merck KGaA Cuts Outlook Amid Macroeconomic Challenges
data.description:
(05:00 GMT) *Merck KGaA 1Q Sales EUR5.28B
(05:00 GMT) *Analysts Saw Merck KGaA 1Q Net Sales at EUR5.28B
(05:00 GMT) *Merck KGaA 1Q Ebitda Before One-Time Items EUR1.535B
(05:00 GMT) *Analysts Saw Merck KGaA 1Q Ebitda Before One-Time Items EUR1.51B
(05:00 GMT) *Merck KGaA 1Q EBITDA EUR1.48B
(05:00 GMT) *Merck KGaA 1Q EBIT EUR1.01B
(05:00 GMT) *Merck KGaA Cuts 2025 View
(05:00 GMT) *Merck KGaA 1Q After-Tax Profit EUR738M
(05:00 GMT) *Merck KGaA 1Q Adj EPS EUR2.12
(05:00 GMT) *Analysts Saw Merck KGaA 1Q Adj EPS at EUR2.17
(05:00 GMT) *Merck KGaA Now Sees 2025 EBITDA Before One-Time Items Between EUR5.8B and EUR6.4B
(05:00 GMT) *Merck KGaA Had Seen 2025 EBITDA Before One-Time Items Between EUR6.1B and EUR6.6B
(05:00 GMT) *Merck KGaA Now Expects 2025 Net Sales Between EUR20.9B and EUR22.4B
(05:00 GMT) *Merck KGaA Had Seen 2025 Net Sales Between EUR21.5B and EUR22.9B
(05:00 GMT) *Merck KGaA: Guidance Cut Reflects Impact of Current Macro-Economic, Geopolitical Environment
(05:10 GMT) Merck KGaA Cuts Outlook Amid Macroeconomic Challenges

By Helena Smolak

Germany's Merck KGaA slashed its full-year outlook on the back of challenging macroeconomic conditions and foreign-exchange headwinds.

The life-sciences and electronics company said Thursday that it now expects full-year sales to come in between 20.9 billion and 22.4 billion euros ($23.35 billion-$25.03 billion), previously forecast at between 21.5 billion and 22.9 billion euros. It estimates its earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization before one-time items of 5.8 billion euros to 6.4 billion euros, prior seen at between 6.1 billion euros to 6.6 billion euros.

Its Ebitda before one-time items--the company's preferred profitability metric--rose 5.6% to 1.535 billion euros in the first quarter, as sales rose 3.1% to 5.28 billion euros. The company attributed the increase to temporarily reduced research and development expenses in its healthcare segment and strict cost discipline.

Analysts expected pre-items Ebitda at 1.51 billion euros on net sales of 5.28 billion euros, according to consensus estimates provided by the company.

The company's adjusted earnings per share stood at 2.12 euros, compared with 2.06 euros in the prior-year period, below analysts' expectations.

Its profit after tax rose to 738 million euros from 699 million euros in the prior year period.

"We've slightly adjusted our guidance but continue to remain confident that we are well-positioned to achieve sustainable growth for 2025 and beyond," its chief executive officer Belen Garijo said.

Write to Helena Smolak at helena.smolak@wsj.com

data.title: Allianz posts 2.1% fall in Q1 net profit but confirms 2025 target
data.description:
Germany's Allianz on Thursday posted a worse-than-expected 2.1% fall in first-quarter net profit but confirmed its target for the full year.

Net profit attributable to shareholders of 2.423 billion euros ($2.71 billion) in the three months through March compares with profit of 2.475 billion euros a year earlier. The figure fell short of a 2.702 billion euro consensus forecast.

A one-time tax provision for the sale of a venture in India pressured results, the company said.

For 2025, the company expects operating profit of between 15 billion euros and 17 billion euros, compared with 16.0 billion euros in 2024.

($1 = 0.8937 euros)

data.title: Avolta posts higher quarterly sales growth, shrugs off North America slowdown
data.description:
Swiss travel retailer Avolta posted a 5.3% organic increase in first-quarter turnover on Thursday, driven by its diversified business structure, despite headwinds in North America from lower traffic volumes earlier in the year.

The Basel-based company also confirmed its full-year outlook and mid-term targets.

The company, which runs shops at airports, on cruise liners, in seaports, and other tourist locations worldwide, posted a core turnover of 3.05 billion Swiss francs ($3.63 billion) for the reported quarter, up from 2.78 billion francs a year earlier.

Avolta's stock has climbed 23% since the start of the year, fuelled by investor confidence in its strategic diversification across various channels and regions, particularly amid uncertainty in the U.S. travel market.

Amid Avolta's growth momentum, U.S. consumer confidence recorded its sharpest drop in nearly five years this April, marking the sixth straight monthly decline.

This downward trend has been compounded by persistently high inflation and mounting concerns over the economic impact of potential tariffs, which are clouding the broader economic outlook.

"While North America faced headwinds due to lower traffic volumes in Q1 2025, performance in other regions more than compensated," Chief Executive Officer Xavier Rossinyol said in a statement.

"We are actively monitoring the geopolitical evolution, mitigating against potential impacts as needed with a continued focus on growth and profitability," Rossinyol added.

In North America, core turnover for the quarter reached 991 million Swiss francs, up from 974 million francs in the same period last year.

The region remains Avolta's second-largest market, accounting for 32% of the group's total turnover in 2024.

($1 = 0.8394 Swiss francs)
